what_it_does: Acts as a verification-driven completion checklist (truth table) for two sequential campaigns: finishing the resolution core (RC-A through RC-D) and then the mobile major upgrade (M1 through M-close plus an ongoing form review), recording what was built, how it was verified, honest gaps, and deferred backlog items.
observations: Each criterion is split into FUNCTION and FORM subsections and marked with [x] only when verified by actual use (proofs run, browser driven).
The document opens with a BINDING DISCIPLINE listing six recurring principles such as 'Derive, never place', 'The system, not the use', and 'Verify by use; honest deviations stated, never laundered'.
Phase 1 (RC-A to RC-D) covers typed relationship edges, a unified role resolver, dials targeting roles, and foundation cleanup; most items are marked done but carry explicit deferred flags.
Phase 2 (M1 to M-close) covers mobile redesign: one-line topbar, WORK|PANEL viewer, orientation reflow, re-fillable typed panel, scrubber, controls-as-records, and an estate-readiness check.
A long M-FORM-REVIEW section records live, iterative feedback from Tim (mob-9 through mob-24) that reopened the mobile form and produced many autonomous fixes plus several Tim-gated decisions.
Every done claim is paired with 'HONEST' deviations or 'BACKLOG' follow-ons rather than being presented as fully closed.
The document references source material by path/section (docs/RESOLUTION.md, docs/RELATIONSHIPS.md, dna/roles.json, docs/analysis/2026-06-13-mobile-ui.md) but does not reproduce their contents.
It uses a tagging convention: rcore-<n> for resolution-core work and mob-<n> for mobile work, each dated.
It repeatedly states that the stage close is Tim's to feel, not the author's to declare.
standouts: The hard rule that checks go green only when verified by use — never by reading code.
The 'honest deviations stated, never laundered' discipline applied to nearly every completed criterion.
The M-FORM-REVIEW turning the document from a static checklist into a live, back-and-forth redesign log driven by Tim's sight-based feedback.
The recurring tension between building a general mechanism ('the system, not the use') and the specific mobile viewer being redesigned.
conventions: Criterion IDs follow a strict dependency order: RC-A → RC-B → RC-C → RC-D, then M1…M-close.
Each criterion uses FUNCTION / FORM headings and ends with [x] by use or [ ] by use markers.
Deferred work is flagged with ⚑ or 'HONEST' and routed to docs/BACKLOG.md.
Tim decisions are called out explicitly with dates and quoted direction.
Code-level identifiers (DNA.resolve, DNA.splitPane, DNA.pinchZoom, dna/types.json, etc.) are used as evidence anchors.
CSS custom properties are referenced with the --dna-* naming convention.
concerns: The document is extremely long and mixes completed work, open work, principles, and self-reported verification in a single stream, which may obscure current state.
Many items are 'verified by use' through browser developer tools or synthetic events rather than automated tests, so reproducibility depends on the author's notes.
A large number of mobile iterations (mob-9 through mob-24) suggest the original M-close readiness claim was premature.
Several 'honest gaps' are repeatedly carried forward rather than resolved, raising the risk of accumulation.
Some verification claims reference screenshots sent to Tim that are not present in the file.
notes: A self-auditing, use-verified completion ledger for the resolution core and mobile viewer redesign, notable for recording honest gaps and Tim-gated decisions alongside each done item.
questions: What are the exact contents and schemas of docs/RESOLUTION.md, docs/RELATIONSHIPS.md, dna/roles.json, and docs/analysis/2026-06-13-mobile-ui.md?
What are the seven proofs run by ./engine/rebuild.sh and how is green defined?
What is the precise scope of the 'live-composed work' backlog item that is gated on Tim's pick?
What does the final mobile quality bar look like from Tim's perspective, given the document says the stage is not closed?
How will the Atlas chip be integrated into the roombar without affecting desktop pixel stability?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be the truth table / completion criteria for finishing the core and mobile upgrade; actual content matches that but has grown into an extensive iterative work log and backlog tracker with live form-review commentary.
apparent_themes: Derive values from data, never place them by hand.
Build general mechanisms rather than one-off features.
Verify completion by running code and using the browser, not by reading code.
Record deviations and deferred decisions honestly instead of laundering them.
Ground names and relationships in real source material rather than inventing them.
Keep the mobile surface navigable and visual with minimal on-screen text.
Maintain desktop pixel stability while redesigning mobile.
intention_signals: The file is trying to enforce a transparent, non-faking delivery process where every implementation claim is tied to observable proof, every shortcut is flagged, and design choices trace back to recorded sources and Tim's explicit direction rather than author invention.
novelty: The explicit 'verify by use, never by reading code' completion rule combined with a structured 'honest deviations / never laundered' accounting method, applied to both a typed role/edge resolver and a live mobile UI redesign in one document.
contribution: A structured, self-auditing rubric that links each build claim to a verification method, records unresolved decisions and follow-ons, and encodes delivery principles (derive-by-data, system-not-use, verify-by-use) that a fused system could keep to maintain transparency.
summary_for_embedding: A verification-driven completion-criteria document for the resolution-core and mobile-major-upgrade campaigns, listing RC/M criteria with FUNCTION and FORM checks, 'verify by use' evidence, honest gaps, backlog flags, and a live Tim-directed mobile form-review trail.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the truth table and honest progress ledger for finishing the resolution core and rebuilding the mobile viewer, ensuring every claim is proven by use and every deviation is recorded rather than laundered.
